methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing bryozoan packstone samples for analysis.

steps.description: Twenty-two samples of approximately 200 g were collected for analysis from two bryozoan packstone beds with thin marly layers.
steps.searchable_summary: collect 22 samples of bryozoan packstone beds

steps.description: Sixty grams of each sample were placed in a drying.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the abundance and distribution of benthic foraminifers in the samples.

steps.description: All observed benthic foraminifers are calcareous with a hyaline test. Approximately 85% of the benthic foraminifers were identified to the order level and 80% to genus level.
steps.searchable_summary: identify benthic foraminifers to order and genus level

steps.description: Statistical analyses were carried out on a reduced genus group, where genera comprising less than 5% were discarded.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis on reduced genus group

steps.description: The five dominating genera - Cibicides, Anomalinoides, Osangularia, Patellina and Spirillina - which each constitute more than 5% of the total benthic foraminiferal assemblage were treated statistically.
